title: Its Never Too Late For Now

jokes.text: Hang on. Why do you have a cat? And a fanny pack? And your ponytail, it's being held up by a chip clip.
jokes.setup: Liz announces her breakup but appears disheveled
jokes.punchline: Enumeration of increasingly pathetic accessories culminating in chip clip as hair tie
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of visual comedy details that paint a vivid picture of Liz's downward spiral. The chip clip detail is unexpectedly specific and pathetic.

jokes.text: I've had three chances... Floyd, then Carol, and I was once in an elevator with Tom Brokaw, and I blew all three... Opportunities!
jokes.punchline: Tom Brokaw as romantic prospect, then clarification of 'blew' meaning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection that sets up sexual innuendo then immediately clarifies. Tom Brokaw as romantic prospect is absurd but fits Liz's character.

jokes.text: I have adopted this cat, named her Emily Dickinson... Oh, come on! Named her Emily Dickinson.
jokes.setup: Liz names her cat after famous recluse poet
jokes.punchline: Jenna's exasperated reaction to the obvious spinster choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character choice - Emily Dickinson is the archetypal spinster poet. Jenna's interruption shows how predictable Liz's choice is.

jokes.text: Recent breakup, fanny pack, cat? Quick. Who is the lead character on NCIS?
jokes.setup: Jack notices signs of Liz giving up on life
jokes.punchline: NCIS knowledge as diagnostic test for spinster descent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant shorthand for cultural decline - NCIS knowledge as ultimate indicator of giving up. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I took the money I was saving for my honeymoon, and I bought a cemetery plot.
jokes.setup: Liz explains her post-breakup financial decisions
jokes.punchline: Honeymoon money redirected to death preparation
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly logical for depressed Liz. The practical reallocation of funds from love to death is bleakly funny.

jokes.text: You know there's a movie of that, right? I did not.
jokes.setup: Liz mentions reading Murder on the Orient Express
jokes.punchline: Liz's obliviousness to the famous film adaptation
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character beat showing Liz's cultural isolation. Her genuine surprise is endearing.

jokes.text: Since inventing democracy, those guys have been coasting.
jokes.punchline: Dismissing 2500 years of history as coasting since democracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line - dismissive of entire civilizations with corporate efficiency language. Maximum comedy per word.

jokes.text: We're calling her Liddy, after Liddy Dole, G. Gordon Liddy, and my martial arts instructor Li Di.
jokes.punchline: Three wildly different Liddy/Li Di inspirations including Watergate criminal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack absurdity - naming baby after politician, criminal, and martial arts instructor equally. Shows his bizarre cultural references.

jokes.text: Meeting magazine is already calling it 'the first great meeting of the decade.'
jokes.punchline: Magazine devoted entirely to rating meetings
jokes.explanation: Absurd but believable in Jack's corporate world. The specificity of 'first great meeting of the decade' is perfectly pretentious.

jokes.text: It's okay. Don't worry. You just keep watching Bridalplasty. Alysinna died last week.
jokes.punchline: Dismissal via reference to reality TV death
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of both corporate dismissal of public interest and reality TV. The casual mention of death is effectively disturbing.

jokes.text: These women run your household, so you have to keep them happy, which means not saying anything as your DVR fills up with Trinidadian soap operas.
jokes.punchline: Specific consequence of appeasing Caribbean caregivers
jokes.explanation: Specific and relatable upper-class problem. The Trinidadian soap opera detail feels authentic and observed.

jokes.text: I can fit Emily Dickinson's whole head in my mouth.
jokes.setup: Liz demonstrates she doesn't need help
jokes.punchline: Disturbing cat-mouth demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disturbing escalation of Liz's decline. Visual is both specific and horrifying. Maximum cringe achieved.

jokes.text: ♪ Everybody's workin' for the weekend ♪ What? Yep. I was in Loverboy.
jokes.setup: Frank asks what band Pete was in
jokes.punchline: Pete was in extremely cheesy 80s band Loverboy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character revelation. Loverboy is the exact right level of cheesy for Pete's rock past. Sets up running gag nicely.

jokes.text: I had to choose between staying in the band or taking that college scholarship to study TV budgeting.
jokes.punchline: Mundane TV budgeting degree vs rock stardom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pete character moment - choosing the most boring possible career path over rock stardom. Very specific and believable.

jokes.text: But let's just say you're at the market, buying potatoes. And that ten-pound bag of potatoes costs... $400.
jokes.setup: Jack tries to explain negotiation with analogy
jokes.punchline: Completely unrealistic potato prices revealing his wealth bubble
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing Jack's complete disconnect from normal economics. The specific $400 price is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: So, what you wan' do? / It was nice negotiating with you, and, uh, here is all of your money.
jokes.setup: Jack tries to negotiate with nanny
jokes.punchline: Complete capitulation after weak attempt
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Jack's rare defeat. The immediate surrender after posturing is effectively humiliating.

jokes.text: I am not gonna just sit and watch you plummet into spinsterhood. Why are you talking so fast? Because I'm upset! Also I've been taking these new Czechoslovakian organ-slimming pills.
jokes.punchline: Czechoslovakian organ-slimming pills explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna absurdity. 'Czechoslovakian organ-slimming pills' is wonderfully specific and bizarre. Sets up meth reveal.

jokes.text: They contain a little bit of meth, which is something my body needs anyway.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of meth as bodily need
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Jenna moment. Treating meth addiction as vitamin deficiency is perfectly in character and disturbing.

jokes.text: I like to do it with an NBA player, because it's fun wordplay and they're mean.
jokes.punchline: Basketball rebound pun plus casual cruelty preference
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with basketball rebounding. 'They're mean' as selling point is perfectly shallow Jenna logic.

jokes.text: I didn't give up when Eric Roberts abandoned me in the desert, and I won't now. No judgment, Liz. Mr. Roberts thought I was dead.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions Eric Roberts abandoning her
jokes.punchline: Clarification that he thought she was dead
jokes.explanation: Great Jenna backstory absurdity. The clarification makes it somehow more reasonable while staying bizarre.

jokes.text: I like my tampons to be cold.
jokes.setup: Liz explains where her ibuprofen is
jokes.punchline: Bizarre tampon storage preference
jokes.explanation: Perfectly uncomfortable Liz overshare. Completely unexpected and specific enough to be memorable.

jokes.text: If I had done that during a mock negotiation in business school, professor Widmer would have spanked me in front of the whole class, bare bottom.
jokes.punchline: Disturbingly specific spanking punishment fantasy
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Jack overshare with BDSM undertones. The specificity makes it particularly cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: Professor Widmer would have given you a 'good job' spanking.
jokes.punchline: Different type of business school spanking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the spanking concept. Distinguishing punishment from reward spankings is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: But you do, right? Yes. But Sherry can't prove that I love Liddy, so I renegotiate under new conditions, specifically, that I hate my newborn daughter.
jokes.punchline: Pretending to hate his baby as leverage
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Jack logic. Treating parental love as negotiation weakness is perfectly corporate sociopathy.

jokes.text: I could never pretend to hate my beautiful baby daughter, Emily Dickinson the cat.
jokes.punchline: Refers to cat as baby daughter
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier cat adoption. Shows how deep Liz has fallen into spinster role-playing.

jokes.text: I could never pretend to hate my beautiful baby daughter, Emily Dickinson the cat.
jokes.setup: Liz responding to Jack's baby hatred plan
jokes.punchline: She calls her cat her 'baby daughter' completely seriously
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Liz's complete absorption in cat-motherhood. The sincerity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: She is one of two people ever to have thrown up on me, and I haven't spoken to Joe Namath since that Mardi Gras.
jokes.setup: Jack explains why he doesn't care about his baby
jokes.punchline: Joe Namath vomiting incident comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack celebrity name-drop with bizarre specificity. Treating baby and football legend equally is brilliant.

jokes.text: I don't think Liddy looks like me, so evolutionarily, that makes me want to eat her.
jokes.setup: Jack continues explaining lack of baby attachment
jokes.punchline: Evolutionary cannibalism logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy. Applying evolutionary psychology to justify baby-eating is perfectly disturbing Jack logic.

jokes.text: Please stay. I'll send everyone in your family to college.
jokes.punchline: Desperate overcompensation offer
jokes.explanation: Good cringe escalation. Jack going from tough negotiator to desperately generous is effective character comedy.

jokes.text: I participated in hands across America.
jokes.punchline: 1980s charity event as credential
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna character moment. Treating charity participation as ID is appropriately narcissistic.

jokes.text: Oh, the color is actually called 'grandfather's shoe.'
jokes.punchline: Unglamorous hair dye name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz response to compliment. 'Grandfather's shoe' is wonderfully unromantic hair color name.

jokes.text: That's my drink! I keep a thermos of it by my toilet!
jokes.punchline: TMI about toilet beverage storage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz overshare. The toilet thermos detail is specific enough to be disturbing but believable.

jokes.text: I participated in hands across America.
jokes.setup: Using ID to prove age for alcohol
jokes.punchline: Referencing a 1986 charity event as proof of age/importance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment using charity participation as celebrity credentials. Very specific to her character's delusions.

jokes.text: Seriously, why can't droids go into the cantina? I mean, Ponda Baba can try to shoot Luke... but a protocol droid is somehow a problem.
jokes.punchline: Logical inconsistency in cantina discrimination
jokes.explanation: Decent nerdy observation. Not groundbreaking but shows character compatibility with Liz.

jokes.text: Ew, Julia Roberts in a movie about eating? Give me Kirstie Alley, somebody who knows what she's doing.
jokes.punchline: Kirstie Alley as superior eating actress
jokes.explanation: Good Liz fat-shaming humor. Dark but fits her character's casual cruelty about weight.

jokes.text: My heart's pounding like I'm watching Oprah's farewell season.
jokes.punchline: Oprah farewell as peak emotional experience
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific simile. Oprah farewell as ultimate emotional benchmark fits Liz perfectly.

jokes.text: Some helpless, pathetic, useless thing that would die if left alo... Oh, God, I do have that. I have NBC.
jokes.setup: Jack describes what Sherry has that he doesn't
jokes.punchline: Realizes NBC is his helpless baby
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Jack realizes NBC is his vulnerable dependent. Perfect character breakthrough.

jokes.text: Oh, way to go, Liz! Attagirl! Walk of shame! You bow-legged bitch!
jokes.setup: Jenna congratulates Liz on one-night stand
jokes.punchline: Supportive encouragement turning to insult
jokes.explanation: Good Jenna escalation. Starting supportive then devolving to random insult is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I once saw an Internet video of a mouse and a bird that are friends.
jokes.setup: Jack tells Liz not to overthink coincidences
jokes.punchline: Completely banal example of 'stranger things'
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimactic Jack example. Mouse and bird friendship as pinnacle of strangeness is wonderfully mundane.

jokes.text: A vast conspiracy involving multiple agents and precise, coordinated efforts.
jokes.setup: Liz considers complex explanation for her night
jokes.punchline: Murder mystery parody structure begins
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody setup of murder mystery structure. Meta-commentary on sitcom plotting conventions.

jokes.text: Then why is my ibuprofen bottle still sealed? Ah.
jokes.punchline: Detective revelation about fanny pack access
jokes.explanation: Good detective work callback. The sealed bottle revelation works within the mystery parody structure.

jokes.text: What, this? I got hit by a bird on a roller coaster.
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific injury explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth absurdity. Bird on roller coaster is specific enough to be believable for his character.

jokes.text: You had to sign your crime, didn't you? You're the one who gave me those moDVDs.
jokes.punchline: DVD gift as evidence of guilt
jokes.explanation: Good detective revelation. Jack's pride leading to exposure fits his character well.

jokes.text: She calls it... Funky juice.
jokes.setup: Toofer describes Liz's drink preference to Anders
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing nickname for wine mixture
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier drink. 'Funky juice' as name is appropriately embarrassing for Liz.

jokes.text: He certainly wasn't a Swiss prostitute that Martha Stewart recommended to me.
jokes.setup: Liz wonders about Anders' true identity
jokes.punchline: Martha Stewart prostitute recommendation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna absurdity placed in Liz's mouth. Martha Stewart as madam is wonderfully specific and unexpected.

jokes.text: Oh, God, a hawk got her! Emily Dickinson!
jokes.setup: Liz makes peace with her life choices
jokes.punchline: Cat immediately dies dramatically
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy timing. Cat death immediately after character growth is brilliantly cruel and unexpected.

jokes.text: ♪ Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, unh, unh, unh. ♪
jokes.punchline: Lazy weekday enumeration with grunts
jokes.explanation: Decent callback to their band subplot. The lazy lyrics show their musical limitations.

tmdb_overview: Jenna tries to help a lovelorn Liz rebound from a broken relationship. Elsewhere, sleep deprivation impacts new father Jack, who begins to lose his business savvy and negotiating prowess; and Pete and Frank form a rock band.
insight: Absurdist character comedy sustains 88-point score across 47 rapid jokes despite minimal standout moments.
